title: Gas report - Week 18
summary:
LNG
The LNG freight market firmed significantly this week, particularly in the Atlantic basin, as fresh fixture activity and chartering demand drove rates higher. Pacific markets also showed resilience, though gains were more modest. On the BLNG1 Gladstone-Tokyo route, 174k cbm vessels rose $400 to $22,800 per day, while 160k cbm vessels held steady at $13,800 per day.

children.title: LNG
children.summary: The LNG freight market firmed significantly this week, particularly in the Atlantic basin, as fresh fixture activity and chartering demand drove rates higher. Pacific markets also showed resilience, though gains were more modest.

children.title: LPG
children.summary: It was a quiet week in the LPG freight market, with rates trending marginally lower across all major routes. Market activity remained subdued, and unless the arbitrage improves, pressure is likely to remain on freight values.
